opportunities.title: NUZEN Agri-Startup Grant for Student Innovators – NaaViC (ICAR-NIVEDI)
opportunities.provider: NaaViC, ICAR-NIVEDI (National Institute of Veterinary Epidemiology and Disease Informatics)
opportunities.description: Up to ₹4L grant-in-aid for student entrepreneurs building agri-startups from idea to prototype , NaaViC, ICAR-NIVEDI.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to aspiring student entrepreneurs from recognised academic institutions Must be working on an agri-innovation idea at the idea to prototype stage Focus on agriculture and allied sectors; veterinary, animal health, and agritech solutions particularly encouraged given ICAR-NIVEDI's mandate No revenue or prior incorporation required Open to aspiring student entrepreneurs from recognised academic institutions Must be working on an agri-innovation idea at the idea to prototype stage Focus on agriculture and allied sectors; veterinary, animal health, and agritech solutions particularly encouraged given ICAR-NIVEDI's mandate No revenue or prior incorporation required Student Founder
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹4 Lakhs as grant with no equity dilution. AmountUpto ₹4 LakhsFund TypeGrantEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableWhat You GetSelected student entrepreneurs receive a grant-in-aid of up to ₹4 lakh for prototype development and early-stage validation. The grant is non-dilutive. Participants also receive mentorship and entrepreneurial support from NaaViC and access to ICAR-NIVEDI's research and scientific ecosystem. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipIncubationApplication FeeFreeBootstrap FriendlyYes
opportunities.timeline: Submit the application via the Google Form at the NUZEN programme link Provide details on your innovation idea, target problem, current stage, and intended use of the grant Applications reviewed by the NaaViC/ICAR-NIVEDI team for innovation potential and sector fit Shortlisted applicants evaluated through the programme's selection process Selected students…Application FeeFree Submit the application via the Google Form at the NUZEN programme link Provide details on your innovation idea, target problem, current stage, and intended use of the grant Applications reviewed by the NaaViC/ICAR-NIVEDI team for innovation potential and sector fit Shortlisted applicants evaluated through the programme's selection process Selected students receive up to ₹4 lakh grant-in-aid along with mentorship and entrepreneurial support For more information, visit naavic. org or nivedi. com, or call 080-23093166 Contact InformationPhone:+91-080-23093166
opportunities.about: The NUZEN Programme is a grant-in-aid initiative by NaaViC, the startup and innovation support arm of ICAR-NIVEDI (National Institute of Veterinary Epidemiology and Disease Informatics), a premier research institute under the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR). The programme is designed specifically for aspiring student entrepreneurs with innovative ideas in agriculture and allied sectors, providing financial and mentorship support to take concepts from the idea stage to a working prototype. Selected participants receive a grant-in-aid of up to ₹4 lakh to fund prototype development and early-stage validation. The programme reflects ICAR-NIVEDI's mandate to translate veterinary and agricultural research into real-world entrepreneurial applications , making it a strong fit for students working on innovations in animal health, veterinary diagnostics, agritech, and allied agricultural domains, though the programme is open to broader agri-innovation ideas. NaaViC provides participants with entrepreneurial support throughout the programme , mentorship, access to ICAR-NIVEDI's research infrastructure, and guidance from scientists and domain experts embedded in India's agricultural research ecosystem. This institutional backing from a central government research body gives student grantees both credibility and access to technical knowledge that is difficult to obtain at an early stage outside academic or government institutions. For student founders with agri-innovation ideas who are at the ideation or early prototype stage, NUZEN offers a rare combination of non-dilutive grant funding and direct access to ICAR's scientific network , particularly valuable for innovations that require domain-specific validation or technical guidance rooted in agricultural science.

opportunities.title: Flagship Accelerator
opportunities.provider: 500 Global
opportunities.description: Silicon Valley-based accelerator with $150K investment for early-stage startups.
opportunities.eligibility: for the 500 Global Flagship Accelerator is specifically designed to attract and support startups poised for significant growth. Applicants must be considered early-stage, which generally implies they have a minimum viable product (MVP) and potentially some initial user traction or early revenue. The focus on early-stage companies allows 500 Global to provide foundational support that can most effectively shape their trajectory. Founders are also required to commit fully to the rigorous four-month in-person program, which is hosted in the heart of Silicon Valley. This in-person requirement is crucial for maximizing the benefits of the intensive curriculum, direct mentorship, and unparalleled networking opportunities available within the Silicon Valley ecosystem. The accelerator fosters a collaborative environment where founders work closely with the 500 Global team and their cohort peers, making physical presence essential for full engagement and impact.

opportunities.about: The 500 Global Flagship Accelerator is a four-month in-person program hosted in Silicon Valley, dedicated to scaling startups to new heights. 500 Global, formerly 500 Startups, pioneered accelerator programs in 2010, shaping how venture capital supports early-stage companies. The firm continues to invest in founders globally, aiming to transform bold visions into disruptive impact through its proven methodology. Companies accepted into the Flagship Accelerator are offered a $150,000 investment in exchange for a 6% equity stake, subject to standard terms and diligence. This capital is intended to provide the crucial foundation needed to build, launch, and quickly grow the startup. The investment helps fuel the founders' vision, allowing them to focus on product development and market expansion. Beyond financial backing, the program delivers comprehensive non-monetary support. This includes founder-focused programming, a growth and development curriculum, and tailored assistance from the 500 Global team of operator-investors. Participants benefit from mentorship provided by industry experts, alumni founder coaches, and entrepreneurs in residence, offering real-world insights into areas like distribution, design, data, and fundraising. The curriculum emphasizes growth hacks and design best practices, covering critical areas such as customer discovery, product-market fit, go-to-market strategies, and pitch development. Founders also gain access to a vast global network of investors and resources, instrumental for market access and leveraging opportunities on a global scale. Additionally, participants receive over $1 million in exclusive credits and discounts on various tools through the 500 FounderHub. 500 Global is a venture capital firm with a mission to discover and back the world's most talented founders, helping them create successful companies at scale. Established in 2010 as 500 Startups, it quickly grew into a global venture capital powerhouse, investing in over 2,800 companies across 80+ countries. Their portfolio includes numerous unicorns and successful exits, demonstrating a strong track record in identifying and nurturing high-potential startups across various industries. The firm's deep expertise spans technology sectors, leveraging a network of mentors and global resources to support founders from idea to IPO.

opportunities.title: Alchemist Chicago
opportunities.provider: Alchemist, Polsky Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation
opportunities.description: for pre-seed/seed deep tech startups with technical founding teams.
opportunities.eligibility: To be considered for the Alchemist Chicago accelerator program, applicants must meet several stringent criteria: Startups must be at the pre-seed or seed-stage, as the program is structured for early-stage deep tech companies. Focus must be on deep tech innovations, covering areas like AI, quantum computing, biotech, and other frontier…To be considered for the Alchemist Chicago accelerator program, applicants must meet several stringent criteria: Startups must be at the pre-seed or seed-stage, as the program is structured for early-stage deep tech companies. Focus must be on deep tech innovations, covering areas like AI, quantum computing, biotech, and other frontier technologies. Founding teams must be technical and comprise at least two co-founders, demonstrating the capability for complex R&D and robust leadership. Founders must be able to attend Phase 1 of the program in-person in Chicago, essential for direct engagement with university resources and mentors. Founders must also attend Phase 2, including two retreats, in San Francisco, critical for accessing Silicon Valley's investor network. Global founders are welcome, provided they can obtain appropriate visas and are fluent in English, ensuring seamless participation. Prior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹45 Lakhs as accelerator. AmountUpto ₹45 LakhsFund TypeAcceleratorRepaymentNon-RepayableWhat You GetParticipants receive a ₹4,500,000 SAFE investment (4% equity for Phase 2), plus over $1M in cloud credits/perks from partners like Microsoft, Google. Benefits include co-working space (Chicago/SF), intensive entrepreneurial training, IP strategy, grant-writing support, and access to UChicago's talent/labs. Phase 2 offers investor access, fundraising support, mentorship, a global network, commercialization guidance, and lifetime community membership. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipCloud CreditsCo WorkingLegalNetworkingMarket AccessTrainingIncubationDisbursementTranchedProgram Duration6 monthsCohort Size30 startups
opportunities.timeline: The application process for Alchemist Chicago is highly selective, structured in two distinct phases: Up to 30 startups are selected for Phase 1 of the program, undergoing intensive entrepreneurial training, mentorship, and validation within the University of Chicago ecosystem. From Phase 1, up to 10 companies demonstrating sufficient progress and…The application process for Alchemist Chicago is highly selective, structured in two distinct phases: Up to 30 startups are selected for Phase 1 of the program, undergoing intensive entrepreneurial training, mentorship, and validation within the University of Chicago ecosystem. From Phase 1, up to 10 companies demonstrating sufficient progress and potential advance to Phase 2, unlocking further resources and investment. Phase 1 concludes with a final showcase event, where participating startups present their refined strategies to mentors and potential investors. Phase 2 culminates in Alchemist's Demo Day, a pivotal event where advanced companies pitch their ventures to leading venture capitalists, corporate partners, and industry stakeholders, aiming for follow-on funding and market partnerships.
opportunities.about: The Alchemist Chicago accelerator program serves as a premier launchpad for foundational and deep tech innovation, delivered through a strategic partnership between Alchemist and the Polsky Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation. Alchemist is a globally recognized, venture-backed accelerator focused on enterprise-monetizing seed-stage ventures, providing a structured path to traction and fundraising. The Polsky Center at the University of Chicago advances innovative ideas from its ecosystem to market, supporting entrepreneurs from various backgrounds. This collaboration uniquely bridges world-class scientific research with real-world market impact, combining UChicago’s deep scientific expertise with Alchemist’s extensive global network and market-entry strategies, particularly valuable for deep tech commercialization. The program provides substantial financial backing, offering participants a ₹4,500,000 SAFE investment upon advancing to Phase 2. This capital is crucial for deep tech startups, which often require longer development cycles. Additionally, participants receive over $1 million in partner perks from industry leaders like Microsoft, Google, and IBM, providing access to essential cloud credits, software tools, and technical support. This comprehensive resource package is tailored to accelerate the development and scaling of complex deep tech innovations. For companies selected for Phase 2, participation typically involves granting 4% equity, with flexible terms available to accommodate specific venture needs. Participants in Phase 1 gain unparalleled access to co-working space at the University of Chicago and intensive entrepreneurial training from seasoned professors. Support for IP strategy and grant-writing is also provided, essential for protecting novel technologies and securing non-dilutive funding. Phase 1 participants benefit from direct access to the University of Chicago's student talent and the vast national laboratory ecosystem, enabling advanced R&D. Founders progressing to Phase 2 receive continued premium co-working space in San Francisco, along with crucial investor access, structured fundraising support, and curated introductions to relevant venture capitalists and strategic partners. Key non-monetary support is a cornerstone of the program, including highly individualized mentorship from experienced Alchemist Partners, university professors, and specialized sector experts. The program also provides access to a powerful global network, comprehensive commercialization guidance to navigate complex markets, and a lifetime global community membership for Phase 2 participants. This fosters lasting connections and market access, vital for the long-term success of deep tech ventures. Quantum startups are particularly encouraged to apply to leverage the program's specialized expertise.
